isolation.validatorOwnedSession: attempted via browser registration/login and isolated backend-created account

assertions.title: Direct entry and shell entry converge on the same timer state

steps.action: Read mission/browser guidance and confirm reused runtime readiness on ports 5173 and 8080.
steps.expected: Both services reachable so browser validation can run.
steps.observed: Frontend returned HTTP 200 and backend returned HTTP 404 on HEAD /, confirming reachability.

steps.action: Open the website and authenticate with a validator-owned browser session using the canonical register/login surface.
steps.expected: Successful registration or login redirects to /timer with the authenticated shell visible.
steps.observed: Browser registration and login stayed on unauthenticated auth pages; prior network capture during login showed POST /web/v1/auth/login followed by authenticated timer-related fetches, but the visible document remained /login and subsequent direct /timer navigation redirected back to /login.

steps.action: Create a separate validator-owned account through the backend auth register endpoint to inspect session behavior.
steps.expected: Returned session cookie should support authenticated browser navigation to /timer under the same local runtime.
steps.observed: Backend returned 201 with a Set-Cookie header using Domain=localhost. Injecting that cookie into the browser still resulted in /timer redirecting to /login, so a stable authenticated browser session could not be established for shell-vs-direct comparison.

steps.action: Compare Timer opened through shell navigation versus direct /timer entry under the same authenticated session.
steps.expected: Both paths show the same default calendar timer state, header state, and workspace-scoped facts.
steps.observed: Blocked because the browser session could not remain authenticated on the reused runtime.

evidence.consoleErrors: Browser console repeatedly showed 401 and 403 resource failures on auth/timer attempts; React Grab debug output was also present from the local environment.
evidence.network: Observed POST /web/v1/auth/login plus timer/session fetches in agent-browser request log, but visible navigation stayed at /login. Direct backend register call returned 201 Created with Set-Cookie: opentoggl_session=...; Domain=localhost; Path=/; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax.

assertions.issues: Blocked by inability to establish or preserve a validator-owned authenticated browser session on the reused local runtime, preventing the required shell-vs-direct timer comparison.

frictions.description: The reused runtime exposed a login/register UI, but successful browser auth did not converge to a stable authenticated shell despite authenticated-looking follow-up fetches in the request log.
frictions.resolution: Tried browser register, browser login, backend-created validator account, and manual cookie injection; all still redirected /timer to /login.

blockers.description: Could not obtain a stable authenticated browser session on the reused runtime, so the assigned shell-navigation vs direct-entry assertion could not be exercised.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified HTTP readiness, retried browser register/login flows, inspected request log and console output, created a fresh account directly via POST /web/v1/auth/register, and injected the returned session cookie into the browser session.

summary: VAL-CROSS-002 is blocked on the reused local runtime because the validator-owned browser session could not stay authenticated long enough to compare shell Timer navigation with direct /timer entry.
